source: The production type system (fonts/ + build_design_pdf.py fontspec). All are open-licensed (see fonts/SOURCES.md in the repo).

display.role: Display / titles / logotype. Geometric, techno, wide. All-caps for titles.
display.web_fallback: Orbitron (Google Fonts), then a wide geometric sans, then sans-serif
display.usage: cover titles, section headers, the SPZ wordmark, big airport-shelf-readable type
display.notes: Weight 900 for the loudest titles, 700 for subheads. Tracking slightly open; all-caps.

body.role: Main body + UI text. Warm-geometric grotesque; reads long-form, still feels near-future.
body.web_fallback: Space Grotesk (Google Fonts), then system-ui, then sans-serif
body.usage: body copy, paragraphs, captions, buttons, most running text
body.italic: no true italic shipped — the print pipeline fakes a 0.18 slant (FakeSlant). Prefer weight/color for emphasis over faux-italic on screen.

mono.role: Code / terminal / signal. The hacker voice made visible.
mono.web_fallback: JetBrains Mono, then ui-monospace, then monospace
mono.usage: code blocks, terminal output, timestamps, track IDs, data/metadata, glitch text
mono.color: often set in cyan (#00F0FF) on void to read as 'signal'

serif_alt.role: Alternate serif used on some book covers for a literary/tabloid register (contrast against the neon).
serif_alt.web_fallback: EB Garamond, then Georgia, then serif
serif_alt.usage: optional — book-cover author lines, pull quotes where a serif contrast is wanted. Not part of the core digital system.

hierarchy.title: Orbitron 900, all-caps, pink (#FF1493) on void
hierarchy.subhead: Orbitron 700 or Space Grotesk 700, muted or paper

notes: Three-voice system: Orbitron shouts (display), Space Grotesk speaks (body), JetBrains Mono computes (signal/code).
Titles are LARGE and airport-shelf readable — the covers deliberately over-size the display type.
Fonts live in fonts/ at the repo root; all open-licensed per fonts/SOURCES.md.
